This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: The Sun could scupper Nasa’s fifth attempt to send a rocket to the Moon - telegraph.co.uk
item.description:
1. The Sun could scupper Nasa’s fifth attempt to send a rocket to the Moon  telegraph.co.uk
  2. Astronauts can face 'nearly lethal doses' of solar radiation — so why launch Artemis II during the sun's peak of activity? Space scientist Patricia Reiff explains.  livescience.com
  3. Could a massive solar flare affect today's Artemis II launch?  yahoo.com
  4. All eyes on weather for Artemis II mission sending NC's Christina Koch, other astronauts to moon  wral.com
  5. Powerful X-class solar flare triggers radio blackout ahead of Artemis 2 launch (video)  space.com

item.title: You Know How Scientists Keep Finding Microplastics Literally Everywhere? Well, You'd Never Guess What Their Lab Gloves Are Coated in Straight Out of the Packaging - futurism.com
item.description:
1. You Know How Scientists Keep Finding Microplastics Literally Everywhere? Well, You'd Never Guess What Their Lab Gloves Are Coated in Straight Out of the Packaging  futurism.com

  4. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics in the lab  Phys.org
  5. Contamination from lab gloves casts doubt on microplastics crisis  The Times
